equipment.description: Thick-walled cylindrical steel vessel containing the nuclear reactor core.
Large heat exchanger that converts water into steam from heat produced in a nuclear reactor core.
Vessel used in a pressurized water reactor to control pressure in the primary coolant loop.
Large vertical pump used to circulate primary coolant through the reactor core and steam generators.
Mechanism used to insert or withdraw control rods from the reactor core to control reactivity.
Pump used to spray water into the containment building to reduce pressure and wash out fission products.
Pump used to remove decay heat from the reactor core after shutdown.
High-pressure pump used to inject borated water into the reactor coolant system during an emergency.
Steam-driven high pressure pump for emergency core cooling in a BWR.
Large capacity, low pressure pump for emergency core cooling in a BWR.
Pump used to spray water directly onto the fuel assemblies in the reactor core during an emergency.
Large, fast-closing valve used to isolate the steam line from the containment building.
Steam turbine driven pump used to supply feedwater to the steam generators.
Electric motor driven pump used to supply feedwater to the steam generators.
Vessel used to remove moisture and reheat steam between the high and low pressure turbines.
Large heat exchanger used to condense steam exiting the low pressure turbine.
Pump used to move condensed water from the main condenser to the feedwater heaters.
Large capacity pump used to supply cooling water to the main condenser.
Heat exchanger used to cool intermediate cooling water system.
Pump used to circulate cooling water to various plant components.
Safety-related pump used to supply cooling water from the ultimate heat sink to the plant.
Tank used to store concentrated boric acid solution.
Pump used to transfer concentrated boric acid to the blending system.
Heat exchanger used to cool primary coolant letdown before it enters the purification system.
Heat exchanger used to recover heat from letdown water to preheat charging water.
Tank used to accommodate fluctuations in primary coolant volume and add hydrogen.
High-pressure pump used to return purified coolant to the primary system and provide seal injection to reactor coolant pumps.
Vessel containing ion exchange resins to remove dissolved impurities from the primary coolant.
Pump used to circulate water through the spent fuel pool and its heat exchangers.
Heat exchanger used to remove decay heat from the spent fuel pool water.
Large tank containing borated water used for emergency core cooling and refueling operations.
Positive displacement pump used to inject neutron absorber (sodium pentaborate) into a BWR.
Tank used to store sodium pentaborate solution for emergency shutdown.
Compressor used to maintain pressurized air in receivers for starting emergency diesel generators.

equipment.type: Emergency Diesel Generator Fuel Oil Storage Tank
equipment.description: Underground or protected tank storing fuel oil for emergency diesel generators.

equipment.type: Emergency Diesel Generator Fuel Oil Transfer Pump
equipment.description: Pump used to transfer fuel oil from main storage to the day tanks.
Compressor used to supply clean, dry air for pneumatic instruments and valves.
Compressor used to supply compressed air for general plant services and tools.
Cooling unit located inside containment to maintain ambient temperature during normal operation and post-accident.
Refrigeration machine used to provide chilled water for control room air conditioning.
Tank used to store demineralized, unborated water for plant make-up.
Highly sensitive detector used to monitor reactor neutron flux during shutdown and start-up.
Detector used to monitor reactor neutron flux during reactor heat-up and approach to criticality.
Detector used to monitor reactor power levels during normal power operation.
System used to measure the water level inside the reactor pressure vessel, especially during post-accident conditions.
Transmitter used to monitor the pressure inside the containment building to detect leaks or pipe breaks.
Transmitter used to monitor and control the water level on the secondary side of the steam generator.
Large step-up transformer connecting the main generator to the high-voltage transmission grid.
Transformer used to step down generator voltage to supply power to plant auxiliary equipment.
Electrical distribution equipment typically operating at 4.16kV to 13.8kV for powering large motors.
Electrical distribution equipment typically operating at 480V for powering smaller motors and systems.
Assembly of motor starters and breakers for centralizing the control of 480V electric motors.
Large battery bank providing uninterruptible DC power for critical plant controls and instruments.
Equipment used to charge the station batteries and supply continuous DC loads.
Inverter used to convert 125V DC battery power to reliable 120V AC power for critical instruments.
Large diesel-driven generator used to provide backup power to safety-critical systems upon loss of offsite power.
